key_insight: HACO, a human-AI co-discovery system, found that a masked generative model from vision (MaskGIT) can be adapted into MaskGXT for crystal structure prediction with sparse human guidance.
engineering_relevance: This shows that cross-domain model transfer plus small domain-specific adjustments can improve crystal structure prediction for materials design workflows.
method: Searches across generative modeling ideas from multiple fields to find transferable algorithms.
Recasts crystal structures as discrete tokens and uses a masked generative framework.
Adds crystallographic symmetry tokens, space-group stratified sampling, and sub-bin coordinate refinement.
evidence_signal: On the MP-20 polymorph split, MaskGXT achieved 79.06% METRe accuracy versus 70.87% for the strongest baseline, and it also led on standard MP-20 and MPTS-52 benchmarks.
watch_item: Performance may depend on cheap, fast, and well-aligned validation settings, so broader generalization beyond these benchmarks should be verified.
paper_title: Discovering Crystal Structure Prediction Algorithms with an AI Co-Scientist
